facts_disclosed: Nan's 78th birthday July 5, Caribbean food, church friends, £85 cake

turns.content: so I need help planning a party and I want to get it right because it's for the most important person in my life
That sounds lovely! Tell me more — who's the party for and what kind of vibe are you going for?
it's for my nan. she's turning 78 on July 5 and I want to throw her something proper. she keeps saying 'oh Marcus don't make a fuss' but she's getting a fuss whether she likes it or not you know

Ha, that's the most grandma thing ever — "don't make a fuss" while secretly loving every minute of it. July 5th gives you a good couple of months to plan. What kind of party are you thinking? Garden party, hall hire, restaurant, house party?

I was thinking hire a hall or do it at her church actually. she's got bare friends at the church and they'd all want to come. and Caribbean food obviously — can't do a party for nan without proper Caribbean food. jerk chicken, rice and peas, curry goat, the lot

The church is a great option — familiar space for her, easy for her friends to get to, and usually affordable to hire. Plus there's normally a kitchen you can use for the food.

For the Caribbean food, are you thinking of doing it yourself, getting family to help cook, or hiring a caterer? If it's a big group a caterer might save you a lot of stress on the day.

my aunties would definitely want to cook some of it tbh. that's kind of the tradition — everyone brings a dish. but I might get a caterer for the main stuff like the jerk chicken and curry goat because that's a lot of food to prep. I could do the rice and peas myself actually I've been practising lol

Love that — a mix of family-cooked dishes and catered mains is the best of both worlds. Your nan probably cares more about the family effort than any fancy catering anyway.

For the caterer, if you're in an area with Caribbean restaurants or catering services, get quotes early since July is busy. Most will do per-head pricing for jerk chicken and curry goat.

How many people are you expecting roughly?

probably about 40-50 people? her church friends, family, some neighbours. she's known in the area you know everyone loves her. also I already sorted the cake — there's a bakery over in St Pauls that does proper Bajan cakes so I ordered a rum cake from them. cost me £85 but she's worth it bruv

£85 for a proper rum cake from a Bajan bakery sounds worth every penny — you can't beat the real thing. And 40-50 people is a good size, big enough to feel like a celebration but intimate enough that she can actually talk to everyone.

For that number, here's a rough planning checklist:

- **Venue:** Confirm the church hall booking ASAP, especially if July 5th is a Saturday
- **Caterer:** Get 2-3 quotes for jerk chicken and curry goat for ~50 people
- **Family dishes:** Coordinate with aunties so you don't end up with 5 macaroni pies and no plantain
- **Drinks:** Rum punch, sorrel, soft drinks, maybe a few beers
- **Decorations:** Keep it classy — balloons, tablecloths, maybe some photos of her through the years
- **Music:** Reggae, calypso, soca — whoever's got the speaker and the playlist

Do you want help with any of those specifically?

the photo idea is sick actually. I could put together like a slideshow or something with old photos of her. she came over from the Caribbean in the 60s so there's some proper classic photos from back in the day. she'd cry. in a good way

A photo slideshow would absolutely be the highlight of the party. You could run it on a laptop connected to a TV or projector at the hall. Organise it roughly by decade — early photos from when she first arrived, family milestones, grandkids growing up, recent ones.

If you want to make it extra special, you could ask a few people to record short video messages beforehand — church friends, family who can't make it, maybe grandkids saying what she means to them. Play it during the slideshow. Guaranteed tears.

bruv the video messages idea is actually gonna make me cry and it's not even my birthday. I'm definitely doing that. gonna text my cousins and get them to record something. some of them are still in Barbados so they probably can't come but they can send a video

That's perfect — having the family in Barbados involved through video messages means she gets to hear from everyone even if they can't be there in person. That'll mean the world to her.

Set a deadline for the videos though — say two weeks before — so you have time to put them together. And keep it a surprise if you can. The less she knows about the details the better the reaction will be.

yeah I'm keeping the whole thing low key. she thinks it's just a small dinner lol. my aunty Carol is in on it she's helping coordinate but nan doesn't suspect anything. this is gonna be so good man I can't wait. she's done everything for me you know she deserves the world

She sounds like an incredible woman, and the fact that you're putting this much thought and love into it says everything. She raised you right.

You've got a solid plan: church hall, Caribbean catering plus family dishes, Bajan rum cake, photo slideshow with video messages, surprise element. Just lock in the venue and caterer in the next couple of weeks and the rest will fall into place.

July 5th is going to be special. She's going to love it.
appreciate you bruv fr. I'm booking the hall this week and getting quotes for the catering. gonna make it a day she never forgets. safe for all the ideas
You've got this. She's going to have the best 78th ever. Good luck with the planning — enjoy every bit of it.
